items.md: <naikassociates110@gmail.com>
**Safety in Construction Sectional Committee, CED 45**
items.value: Safety in Construction Sectional Committee, CED 45

items.md: # FOREWORD
items.value: FOREWORD

items.md: This Indian Standard was adopted by the Bureau of Indian Standards, after the draft finalized by the Safety in Construction Sectional Committee had been approved by the Civil Engineering Division Council.
items.value: This Indian Standard was adopted by the Bureau of Indian Standards, after the draft finalized by the Safety in Construction Sectional Committee had been approved by the Civil Engineering Division Council.

items.md: A large number of workmen, skilled and unskilled, are employed in numerous construction works, big and small, under execution in the country. Due to the increased tempo of such works and large scale mechanization, hazards of accidents have increased considerably. It has, therefore, become imperative that adequate safety rules are laid down for every phase of work, and that these are meticulously followed.
items.value: A large number of workmen, skilled and unskilled, are employed in numerous construction works, big and small, under execution in the country. Due to the increased tempo of such works and large scale mechanization, hazards of accidents have increased considerably. It has, therefore, become imperative that adequate safety rules are laid down for every phase of work, and that these are meticulously followed.

items.md: Safety aspects in some of the constructions may assume such a great importance that instead of merely deputing supervisory staff in adequate strength to look after the safety aspects, it may become desirable to have a separate organization to control this important aspect.
items.value: Safety aspects in some of the constructions may assume such a great importance that instead of merely deputing supervisory staff in adequate strength to look after the safety aspects, it may become desirable to have a separate organization to control this important aspect.

items.md: Certain protective barriers in and around buildings are required specially during demolition of buildings and foundation work for the safety of workers and the public in general. It has, therefore, been felt necessary to lay down the safety precautions required to be taken for protective barriers in and around buildings with a view to minimizing the risk of accidents and injuries and accordingly this standard has been brought out. It does not cover special type of protective barriers like safety nets, protective coverings necessitating separate scaffolding.
items.value: Certain protective barriers in and around buildings are required specially during demolition of buildings and foundation work for the safety of workers and the public in general. It has, therefore, been felt necessary to lay down the safety precautions required to be taken for protective barriers in and around buildings with a view to minimizing the risk of accidents and injuries and accordingly this standard has been brought out. It does not cover special type of protective barriers like safety nets, protective coverings necessitating separate scaffolding.

items.md: <naikassociates110@gmail.com>
# Indian Standard
items.value: Indian Standard

items.md: # PROTECTIVE BARRIERS IN AND AROUND BUILDINGS — CODE OF SAFETY
items.value: PROTECTIVE BARRIERS IN AND AROUND BUILDINGS — CODE OF SAFETY

items.md: ### 1 SCOPE
items.value: 1 SCOPE

items.md: This standard lays down the requirements for protective barriers in and around buildings during construction, maintenance, addition and alteration, and demolition with a view to preventing accidents.
items.value: This standard lays down the requirements for protective barriers in and around buildings during construction, maintenance, addition and alteration, and demolition with a view to preventing accidents.

items.md: ### 2 REFERENCE
items.value: 2 REFERENCE

items.md: The Indian Standard IS 13416 (Part 1) : 1992 ‘Preventive measures against hazards at workplaces — Recommendations: Part 1 Falling material hazards prevention’ is a necessary adjunct to this standard.
items.value: The Indian Standard IS 13416 (Part 1) : 1992 ‘Preventive measures against hazards at workplaces — Recommendations: Part 1 Falling material hazards prevention’ is a necessary adjunct to this standard.

items.md: ### 3 GENERAL
items.value: 3 GENERAL

items.md: **3.1** Scaffolding or staging shall be suitable and adequate and so fastened to prevent it from swaying from the building or structure. Partially or fully dismantled scaffolding, staging or barrier shall be removed from the site of work and stacked at suitable place.
items.value: 3.1 Scaffolding or staging shall be suitable and adequate and so fastened to prevent it from swaying from the building or structure. Partially or fully dismantled scaffolding, staging or barrier shall be removed from the site of work and stacked at suitable place.

items.md: **3.2** Adequate precautions shall be taken to prevent danger from electrical installations.
items.value: 3.2 Adequate precautions shall be taken to prevent danger from electrical installations.

items.md: **3.3** Hazardous and flammable materials shall be stacked separately with warning sign boards.
items.value: 3.3 Hazardous and flammable materials shall be stacked separately with warning sign boards.

items.md: **3.4** Hazards due to falling materials shall be prevented as recommended in IS 13416 (Part 1) : 1992
items.value: 3.4 Hazards due to falling materials shall be prevented as recommended in IS 13416 (Part 1) : 1992

items.md: **3.5** No materials on any of the sites of work shall be stacked or placed as to cause danger or inconvenience to any person or the public.
items.value: 3.5 No materials on any of the sites of work shall be stacked or placed as to cause danger or inconvenience to any person or the public.

items.md: **3.6** Necessary lights and cautionary sign boards shall be provided near the protective barriers to avoid accidents.
items.value: 3.6 Necessary lights and cautionary sign boards shall be provided near the protective barriers to avoid accidents.

items.md: ### 4 DEMOLITION OF BUILDING
items.value: 4 DEMOLITION OF BUILDING

items.md: **4.1** Before any demolition work is commenced and also during the progress of work, the requirements given in **4.1.1** to **4.1.4** shall be followed.
items.value: 4.1 Before any demolition work is commenced and also during the progress of work, the requirements given in 4.1.1 to 4.1.4 shall be followed.

items.md: **4.1.1** All roads and open areas adjacent to the work site shall be either closed or suitably protected.
items.value: 4.1.1 All roads and open areas adjacent to the work site shall be either closed or suitably protected.

items.md: **4.1.2** No electric cable or apparatus which is liable to be a source of danger or apparatus used by the operator shall remain electrically charged.
items.value: 4.1.2 No electric cable or apparatus which is liable to be a source of danger or apparatus used by the operator shall remain electrically charged.

items.md: **4.1.3** All practical steps shall be taken to prevent danger to persons employed from risk of fire, explosion, flooding or collapse of structure and such areas shall be properly demarcated.
items.value: 4.1.3 All practical steps shall be taken to prevent danger to persons employed from risk of fire, explosion, flooding or collapse of structure and such areas shall be properly demarcated.

items.md: **4.1.4** Floor, roof or any other part of the building shall not be so overloaded with debris or materials as to render it unsafe.
items.value: 4.1.4 Floor, roof or any other part of the building shall not be so overloaded with debris or materials as to render it unsafe.

items.md: ### 5 FOUNDATION OF BUILDINGS
items.value: 5 FOUNDATION OF BUILDINGS

items.md: Trenches and foundation pits, wherever directed by the Engineer-in-Charge, shall be securely fenced, provided with caution signs and marked with red lights to avoid accidents.
items.value: Trenches and foundation pits, wherever directed by the Engineer-in-Charge, shall be securely fenced, provided with caution signs and marked with red lights to avoid accidents.

items.md: The use of the Standard Mark is governed by the provisions of the *Bureau of Indian Standards Act, 1986* and the Rules and Regulations made thereunder. The Standard Mark on products covered by an Indian Standard conveys the assurance that they have been produced to comply with the requirements of that standard under a well defined system of inspection, testing and quality control which is devised and supervised by BIS and operated by the producer. Standard marked products are also continuously checked by BIS for conformity to that standard as a further safeguard. Details of conditions under which a licence for the use of the Standard Mark may be granted to manufacturers or producers may be obtained from the Bureau of Indian Standards.
